---
title: Add a VideoPlayer
description: How to add a VideoPlayer to your LightNet page.
---

import { Aside } from "@astrojs/starlight/components";

The `VideoPlayer` component is used to display a video on your page. To implement it, use the `Section` component to fit
the video player into your page structure like this:

```astro
---
import { Page, Section, VideoPlayer } from "lightnet/components"
import VideoImage from "../../assets/video-image.webp"

const { t } = Astro.locals.i18n
---
<Page>
  <Section title={t("video.title")}>
    <VideoPlayer url="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1234" />
  </Section>
</Page>
```

<Aside type="tip">
  We highly recommend using a video hosting platform like YouTube or Vimeo
  because they usually provide better performance. Nevertheless, it is also possible to
  host mp4 videos yourself but be aware you need an [additional file
  storage](/deploy/file-storage).
</Aside>

## Reference

The `VideoPlayer` component has the following properties:

### `url`

type: `string` \
example: `"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1234"` \
required: `true`

The source of the video to display. This can be an external link to YouTube or Vimeo, or a link to an
MP4 file hosted on your server. Other links show a fallback that opens the video in an external browser tab.

### `title`

type: `string` \
example: `"Your Great Video"` \
required: `false`

This title is only used for screen readers. For setting a title on top of the video use the `title` of the section.

### `image`

type: `ImageMetadata` \
example: `import VideoImage from "../../assets/video-image.jpg"` \
required: `false`

The image is used as a preview when you use an MP4 video. Supported formats include `jpg`, `png`, and `webp`.
